page: Amazon Web Services r5.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or graphics processing units.

Benchmark data indicates that the r5.12xlarge delivers average performance across most multi-core and memory-intensive operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score fall within the middle 50% tier, as do the Geekbench multi-core and software compilation benchmarks. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with both stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the average tier, supporting steady data throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of the r5.12xlarge makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high single-threaded compute performance. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 12 Gbps. Given the average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and medium-model LLM inference, this instance is configured for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing tasks that benefit from a 384.0 GB memory footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it provides 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place the r5.12xlarge in the average tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance represents a tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks that require a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without demanding top-tier single-threaded compute speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering average multi-core performance for database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and steady multi-core performance for memory-intensive data workloads.
